topStories.description: Iran has been dominating the social media game with memes and videos amid the ongoing war with the US and Israel.

topStories.title: US markets surge after Trump announces he’s postponing strikes on Iran
topStories.description: Major US financial markets opened higher after US President Donald Trump announced he was postponing strikes on Iran.

topStories.title: Pak Attempts To Act Mediator In Iran War, US-Tehran Talks Likely In Islamabad
topStories.description: Pakistan has the second-largest Shia Muslim population after Iran, and it also maintains close relations with Gulf states, including a mutual defence pact signed last year with Saudi Arabia.

topStories.title: Supreme Court questions its registry over delay in listing PIL

topStories.title: Is US-Iran war about to take a new turn? UAE and Saudi Arabia prepare to join conflict, says report
topStories.description: Gulf monarchies, long reluctant to be drawn into open hostilities, are now edging towards active participation as Iran escalates attacks on regional energy infrastructure and asserts claims over the Strait of Hormuz

topStories.title: Explosion at Valero's Port Arthur Refinery sends thick smoke plume into Texas sky | Video
topStories.description: Local authorities in Texas issued an immediate shelter-in-place order on Monday following an explosion at Valero’s Port Arthur refinery, as emergency crews responded to an active fire at the facility.

topStories.title: Petrol, diesel prices on 24 March: Check fuel rates in your city today — Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, others
topStories.description: On Monday, Trump claimed that talks were underway between US and Iran, and said that he would hold off on strikes against Iran's power plants. Following Trump's comments, the price of Brent Crude dipped below $100 before rising to $103. Check petrol, diesel prices in your city on Tuesday, 24 March.

topStories.description: America may be a nation at war, but the president's activities have been a mix of diplomacy and diversions - with the occasional swing toward the surreal.

topStories.title: Texas oil refinery in flames after explosion
topStories.description: Videos show flames and a plume of thick smoke following an explosion at a Valero oil refinery in Port Arthur, Texas.

topStories.title: US bans new foreign-made consumer internet routers
topStories.description: There are almost no major brands of internet routers that are manufactured in the US.

topStories.title: Rebel enters fray amid row over Congress’s choice of candidate in Davangere South bypoll
topStories.description: A Congress by-election in Davangere South faces backlash over candidate choice, prompting a rebel run by Sadiq Pailwan amid minority community discontent.

topStories.title: US-Iran war to end on 9 April? Israeli media claims ‘target date’ set as Tehran ‘reviews’ Washington's proposals
topStories.description: Israeli media reported that the US had set 9 April as the ‘target date’ to end the war with Iran, while US media reported that Tehran had received a message from American mediators as a pre-cursor to talks and was ‘reviewing’ them. The development comes after Trump claimed talks were underway.

dailyNews.title: Markets set for 3–4% relief rally amid cooling war risks
dailyNews.description: Indian stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Monday, with major indices falling sharply and erasing substantial market value. This decline was attributed to escalating West Asian hostilities and global uncertainties. However, a potential relief rally is anticipated for Tuesday following conciliatory remarks from the US President regarding Iran.

dailyNews.title: Senate approves Trump loyalist Markwayne Mullin as next DHS secretary
dailyNews.description: Markwayne Mullin was confirmed Department of Homeland Security secretary, placing the Oklahoma senator in charge of a Trump administration immigration crackdown that has triggered a 37-day funding shutdown of the cabinet agency.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court disposes plea seeking release of Sonam Wangchuk
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court on Monday closed as “infructuous” a petition seeking the release of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, after the Centre revoked his detention under the National Security Act (NSA) on March 14.

dailyNews.title: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief
dailyNews.description: The US senator will now oversee the embattled department after a series of immigration enforcement blunders.

dailyNews.title: Iran US News Live Updates: Tehran now says it's 'reviewing' a US proposal, hours after denying talks with Trump
dailyNews.description: Iran US News Live Updates: A senior Iranian Foreign Ministry official has confirmed that Tehran received a US proposal via mediators and is reviewing it, according to CBS News. Catch LIVE updates with Mint

dailyNews.title: Trying relentlessly to lessen impact of US-Iran war: PM Modi
dailyNews.description: In a statement in Lok Sabha – his first after the war broke out – Modi called the attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz as “unacceptable”.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court weighs public good in post-facto environmental nod debate
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court on Monday observed that the legislature should not be completely “denuded” from granting environmental clearances (EC) to development projects long after they have commenced work, particularly when such a decision leads to serving a higher public good such as establishment of a hospital or a public utility.

dailyNews.title: Carville unloads on Maher, tells him to ‘get your head out of Bari Weiss’s a--’ in heated Trump rant
dailyNews.description: James Carville fires back at Bill Maher and reacts to Trump’s comments on Robert Mueller on Politicon podcast.

dailyNews.title: Department of Education under Trump just took its 'largest' step closer to shutting down
dailyNews.description: Student loan operations from a nearly $1.7 trillion portfolio are being transferred from the Department of Education to the Treasury under a new interagency agreement.

dailyNews.title: Park Police officer shot in Southeast DC suffers non life-threatening injuries as probe unfolds
dailyNews.description: A US Park Police officer was shot in Southeast Washington, D.C., the D.C. Police Union confirmed, prompting road closures near Queens Stroll Place SE.

dailyNews.title: Obama adviser reveals private conversations urging Biden not to run in 2016
dailyNews.description: The University of Virginia’s Miller Center published an interview with Obama strategist David Plouffe, who was tasked with informing Biden that he should not run for president in 2016.

dailyNews.title: Mullin confirmed as DHS chief as lawmakers near solution on shutdown standoff
dailyNews.description: Even with a new DHS chief, Senate Democrats aren't backing down from their demands for stringent reforms to ICE. And now President Donald Trump wants to pair funding with voter ID legislation.

dailyNews.title: Trump's death will be 'like Mardi Gras in New Orleans,' Charlamagne warns after Mueller post
dailyNews.description: Radio host Charlamagne Tha God warned President Donald Trump that people will celebrate his death "like Mardi Gras" after he celebrated Robert Mueller's death.

dailyNews.title: Trump Delays Threat to Iran, but War Negotiations Are in Early Stage
dailyNews.description: President Trump postponed his threat to strike power plants in Iran, citing “productive conversations” with the Iranians. But officials said the talks were in an early stage and not substantive.

dailyNews.title: Trump Sours on British Leader Over Iran War: ‘What If Donald Shouts at Me?’
dailyNews.description: President Trump once called Prime Minister Keir Starmer a friend. But Mr. Starmer’s decision not to join the attacks on Iran has led to merciless mocking by the president.

dailyNews.title: Mullin’s Smooth Confirmation Was a Throwback in the Senate
dailyNews.description: In choosing Senator Markwayne Mullin, who has warm relationships across the Capitol and the political aisle, President Trump was reverting to a bygone tradition.

dailyNews.title: Trump, Who Calls Mail-in Voting ‘Cheating,’ Just Voted by Mail
dailyNews.description: President Trump has long fixated on mail-in-voting to bolster his baseless claims of widespread voter fraud. But he recently used the method in a Florida special election.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration to Pay $1 Billion to Energy Giant to Cancel Wind Farms
dailyNews.description: In exchange, the French company TotalEnergies would invest in oil and natural gas projects in Texas and elsewhere.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Targets Harvard With Two New Investigations
dailyNews.description: The inquiries target antisemitism and admissions policies. The university called it retaliation for refusing to give in to the administration’s demands.

dailyNews.title: ‘Who Doesn’t Love Elvis?’ Trump Takes an Unexpected Detour to Graceland.
dailyNews.description: President Trump mused about whether he could beat Elvis Presley in a fistfight and used a golden Sharpie to sign a replica of one of the singer’s guitars.

dailyNews.title: 3/16: Face the Nation
dailyNews.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the U.S. launches airstrikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen, Margaret Brennan speaks to Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Plus, President Trump's envoy to the Middle East, Steve Witkoff, joins.

dailyNews.title: Trump postpones strikes on Iran's power plants after "productive conversations"
dailyNews.description: Iranian state media said there was no "direct or indirect communication" with President Trump, but a senior Iranian official later indicated they'd received communication from a mediator.

dailyNews.title: DHS funding talks in limbo after Trump insists GOP pass SAVE America Act
dailyNews.description: Senate talks aimed at ending the 38-day DHS shutdown hit a new roadblock after President Trump called on Republicans to hold out for passage of the SAVE America Act.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls off Strait of Hormuz ultimatum as Iran receives U.S. message from mediators
dailyNews.description: As Iran threatens to mine the entire Persian Gulf, President Trump said his deadline for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen is postponed amid negotiations with Tehran.
